methods.description: This method involves using the Global Coupled Ionosphere-Thermosphere-Electrodynamics Model (GCITEM-IGGCAS) to simulate the effects of geomagnetic field, CO2 levels, and solar activity on the thermosphere from 9455 BC to 2015 AD.

steps.description: Use the GCITEM-IGGCAS model to solve energy, momentum, and continuity equations of neutrals and ions between 90 and 600 km altitude.
steps.searchable_summary: run GCITEM-IGGCAS model for ionosphere-thermosphere simulation
outputs: simulation results of neutrals and ions

steps.description: Run each simulation for an interval of 15 days to remove effects of initial conditions, using the final day results for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: process simulation data to remove initial condition effects

steps.description: Use geomagnetic field data from CALS10k.2 and IGRF models, CO2 concentration data from ice cores and Mauna Loa Observatory, and F10.7 index data from sunspot numbers and modern measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch geomagnetic, CO2, and solar activity data

steps.description: Implement four control runs (CR1-CR4) to identify effects of geomagnetic field, CO2, and solar activity on thermospheric evolution.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct control runs to analyze effects on thermosphere

steps.description: Analyze simulation results to determine the influence of CO2, geomagnetic field, and solar activity on thermosphere evolution.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze simulation results for thermosphere influences

steps.description: Visualize global mean and zonal mean neutral density and temperature as a function of altitude and year.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize thermosphere data over time and altitude
